about:
### Professional Overview

For over a decade, I have focused on a single mission: **Bridging the gap between complex engineering and measurable business growth.**

As a **Lead Backend Architect**, I don't just build software that scales; I design systems that **reduce operational risk** and **maximize Return on Investment (ROI)** in high-stakes sectors like **Fintech, HealthTech, and Industrial ERPs**.

#### 🚀 Business Impact & Strategy
Architecture is a financial decision. My expertise in **Node.js, TypeScript, and Distributed Systems** isn't about following trends—it's about **accelerating Time-to-Market by 2x**, slashing cloud infrastructure spend, and ensuring **zero-downtime reliability** for mission-critical operations. I specialize in turning legacy bottlenecks into competitive advantages.

#### 📈 Leadership for Velocity
I have mentored **30+ engineers** across 8 companies, focusing on building high-performance, autonomous teams. I believe my success is measured by the team's ability to ship high-quality features with predictable velocity and minimal technical debt.

#### 📍 Collaboration Ready
Based in **Antalya, Turkey** (transferable work permit). Supporting **EU and North American** time zones for Lead roles or Senior Architectural Consulting—Remote or On-site.

pillars.title: High-Performance Teams
pillars.description: My mentorship focuses on building autonomous, high-performing engineering cultures. I invest in the technical growth of each engineer to ensure they can make Lead-level decisions that align with product goals.

pillars.title: Architectural Alignment
pillars.description: I translate strategic business sessions into a two-stage **ADR process**: ensuring every technical blueprint serves a specific business objective and stakeholder requirement.

pillars.title: Operational Resilience
pillars.description: I maintain a strict focus on proactive technical-debt management. By balancing continuous refactoring with CEO-level priorities, I ensure long-term system health and predictable delivery velocity.

quote: The best code is the one you didn't have to write.

testimonials.text:
Working with Amin onboard was truly a valuable experience for me. What sets Amin apart is his ability to approach problems at their root cause rather than at the surface level. He doesn't settle for quick or temporary fixes; instead, he analyzes issues deeply, challenges assumptions, and moves forward systematically to reach the right solution. Technical discussions with him were always productive, as he brings a strong engineering mindset while remaining open to different perspectives.

He is impact- and results-driven. He does not hesitate to take ownership, focuses on work that truly creates value, and efficiently turns discussions into action. With his analytical thinking, reliability, and solution-oriented approach, he consistently delivers tangible contributions to the teams he is part of.

I have no doubt that Amin will create measurable and sustainable impact in any team he joins. Any team would be fortunate to work with him, and I strongly recommend him.
testimonials.name: Necdet Yiğit Eroğlu
testimonials.summaryDescription: Amin approaches problems at their root cause. He is impact-driven and a strong engineer who creates measurable value.

testimonials.text:
I worked with Amin Abbasi for over three years at Dannie, where I was a full stack developer and Amin started as a senior backend developer before being promoted to a lead role. For the first two years, we worked together on a daily basis and collaborated very closely on core systems.

Amin is an exceptionally strong backend engineer, especially with TypeScript and Node. He has a deep understanding of large scale systems and was instrumental in maintaining and improving huge codebases. His refactoring work significantly improved code quality and long term maintainability, often in areas that required patience, precision, and a strong architectural mindset.

Beyond technical strength, Amin stands out for his character. He is easygoing, caring, and genuinely invested in the people he works with. He is a natural tutor who enjoys sharing knowledge and helping others grow. He consistently asked for my input during problem solving, valued different perspectives, and created a collaborative environment where ideas could be challenged safely.

As a lead, Amin was not afraid to take responsibility too. When things went wrong, he took the hit himself and shielded the team when necessary. He has a strong core, communicates clearly, and balances empathy with accountability. We also learned and grew a lot together while working on WebSocket based systems, tackling complex real time problems as a team.
testimonials.name: Alp Sarıyer
testimonials.summaryDescription: An exceptionally strong backend engineer with deep understanding of large-scale systems and a natural gift for mentoring.

testimonials.text: I had the pleasure of working with Amin for one year at Irantom Company, collaborating on several project teams. As Node.js Back-end Developer, Amin’s mastery for both his core role and the projects that extend beyond it has been critical to the company's growth. While Amin’s work has continued to pay dividends long past his tenure here, I certainly miss working with him every day.
testimonials.name: Mert Mehralian
testimonials.summaryDescription: His mastery of Node.js and distributed systems has been critical to company growth and long-term stability.
